Alert.xml:
<dict>
	<key>MacroActionType</key>
	<string>Alert</string>
	<key>SoundPath</key>
	<string></string>
	<key>Text</key>
	<string>Would you like to continue execution or stop now?</string>
	<key>TimeOutAbortsMacro</key>
	<true/>
	<key>Title</key>
	<string>Keyboard Maestro Alert</string>
</dict>

PromptForUserInput.xml:
<dict>
	<key>Buttons</key>
	<array>
		<dict>
			<key>Button</key>
			<string>OK</string>
		</dict>
		<dict>
			<key>Button</key>
			<string>Cancel</string>
			<key>Cancel</key>
			<true/>
		</dict>
	</array>
	<key>MacroActionType</key>
	<string>PromptForUserInput</string>
	<key>Prompt</key>
	<string>Please enter the details for these variables.</string>
	<key>TimeOutAbortsMacro</key>
	<true/>
	<key>Title</key>
	<string>Untitled</string>
	<key>Variables</key>
	<array/>
</dict>

category_path: Select or Show a Menu Item
Set Front Browser Field to Text
Set Variable to Front Browser Field

_meta.description: Canonical XML templates for KM built-in action types, captured from Keyboard Maestro's own editor via Edit > Insert Action > By Name. Each entry's `xml` is the action's <dict> body with transient keys (ActionUID, creation dates) stripped. `parameter_paths` maps user-facing parameter names (snake_case) to the plist keys inside the captured template, so the substitution engine doesn't need per-action knowledge of KM's plist schema.
